Empires & Puzzles Ginger Review: The Gnome Explorer

Ginger isn't here to bake cookies; she's here to turn your mana into ballistic missiles. As the Nature element's answer to Guardian Hippo, she brings a chaotic, high-damage playstyle that can wipe teams in seconds—if the RNG gods are smiling. We dive deep into her mechanics, her aging stats, and whether she can still hang with the big kids.
Ginger: The Green Machine Gun of Christmas
Ginger: The Gnome Who Brought a Crossbow to a Snowball Fight

Ginger burst onto the scene in December 2023 as part of the Santa's Challenge event, bringing a mechanic that players immediately recognized: she was the "Green Hippo." As a Fast mana speed 5-star Nature hero, her primary role is to turn her allies' special skills into triggers for massive sniper hits. While she lacks the hit-all consistency of Guardian Hippo, she makes up for it with sheer single-target stopping power. She's a Ranger class hero, which is excellent for bypassing defensive buffs, but in the harsh light of 2026, her base stats are starting to look a bit like last year's fruitcake—still dense, but maybe not what you want to reach for first.

Ginger's design is a delightful mix of festive cheer and imminent violence. She’s a gnome explorer with a frantic expression, wielding a crossbow in one hand and a torch in the other, as if she's just realized she forgot to buy a gift for Santa. The art style captures that whimsical yet dangerous vibe of the classic RPG gnome. Compared to her costume, which sees her tangled in wrapping paper, the base version feels more "adventure-ready," even if she looks like she's had about twelve too many espressos.
When we look at Ginger's stats in the current meta, the reality of power creep sets in. With a maxed power (LB2) of around
, she is significantly behind the curve compared to modern heroes who are pushing well past 1500 power.
Her attack stat is decent for her era but pales in comparison to the 1600+ attack stats we see on newer snipers.
Her survivability is her biggest liability. Modern slash attacks and passives can chew through her health pool before she gets her engine running.
If you are running base Ginger, you are doing it for the mechanic, not the raw stats. She is a glass cannon that needs protection.
Ginger's special skill is a fascinating engine of destruction. Here is the breakdown:
She summons Elf Minions for everyone. These are relatively weak (10% HP/Atk) and serve mostly as a small buffer or to enable minion-based synergies. Don't rely on them for survival.
This is the meat of the card. For 3 turns,
an ally casts a special skill, Ginger fires a 500% damage shot at a random enemy.
500% damage is a "kill shot" percentage. If she fires 3-4 times in a turn, she can delete multiple enemies.
It's random. She might nuke a hero with 10 HP left, wasting 499% damage.
She gives a +20% Special Skill damage buff to allies. This is a nice cherry on top, especially for hit-all allies.
, the base skill lacks the critical Mana Generation buff and deals slightly less damage (500% vs 520%). The costume transforms her into a battery

With roughly 2000 HP (LB2), her minions have ~200 HP. They vanish if a stiff breeze hits them.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family has seen updates, but base Ginger misses out on some of the newer toys found on her costume.

The minion stat boost from the family bonus helps slightly, but since you rarely run multiple Winter heroes outside of specific thematic teams (like with Mother North or Pengi), it's often a non-factor.
have the "Frost on Minion Hit" passive that her costume enjoys. This is a significant loss in sustained damage output.

She gets a regeneration buff for the first few turns. It helps with survivability, but honestly, for a hero this squishy in the modern game, a "Dodge" or "Damage Reduction" power would have been far superior.

Upon release, Ginger was hyped as a balanced Guardian Hippo. Players loved the big numbers but were frustrated by the RNG targeting. She found a home in Titan teams and specific "machine gun" raid offenses.
Her mechanic is timeless, but her numbers are not. The 500% hit is still relevant, but her ability to stay alive long enough to use it has diminished.

Base Ginger is no longer a top-tier defense hero. She is too unreliable and squishy. However, on offense, she is still a monster. If you can control the board and fire her alongside a barrage of other fast heroes, she can still wipe modern teams. She is a "Win More" hero—if you are winning the tile game, she ensures you win fast. If you are losing, she can't turn the tide.
The costume adds massive stat boosts (roughly +400 HP and +400 Attack).

Costume switches to Cleric (Mana Shield), which is generally better for a hero who needs to fire to be useful. Base Ranger bypass is nice for damage, but resisting mana control is better for consistency.
The costume adds +15% Mana Generation to allies. This is game-changing, allowing your team to recharge faster and keep the loop going.
The costume is a straight upgrade in almost every measurable way. Use the base version only if you desperately need the Ranger class for a specific quest or trial.
Ginger is a damage dealer who needs to survive.
Keeps her alive long enough to cast. Her skill damage multiplier (500%) is high enough that she hits hard even without attack nodes.
She dies if a sniper looks at her funny.
. Her base stats are too low for the current meta; she needs every point of bulk she can get to function.
Capable of dealing 2000%+ damage in a single turn with the right setup.

Bypassing defensive buffs ensures her 500% hits actually land.
You cannot control who she hits. She often overkills nearly dead heroes or ignores the high-priority threat.
She does nothing on her own. She requires other allies to fire to deal damage.
Base stats make her a liability in prolonged fights.
Minions provide negligible protection against modern damage.
She provides high damage and buffs ally special skill damage. The random targeting doesn't matter when there is only one target.
Clears waves efficiently when paired with other AOE heroes.
Great in a green stack or a "Machine Gun" rainbow team.
Too unreliable and easy to kill.
Ginger shines in "Chain Reaction" teams. You want heroes who are Fast or Very Fast to trigger her ability repeatedly.
Almur (EDD) -> Ginger -> Evelyn/Fogg (EDD) -> El Naddaha -> Caedmon.
Elemental Defense Down ensures Ginger's hits decimate anyone she touches. Firing 4 greens in a row guarantees 4 hits from Ginger.
Mother North -> Ginger -> Pengi -> Glenda -> Krampus.
Ginger provides minions for Pengi. Pengi fires, triggering Ginger. It's a classic Winter family synergy.

(if she can't cast, she is useless).
Pairing Ginger with Slow heroes wastes her potential. Her buff lasts 3 turns; if no one fires in those 3 turns, she did nothing.

If you pulled Ginger early, congrats! She will carry you through map stages. Pair her with your fastest heroes, even 3-stars and 4-stars. Don't worry about her stats yet; 500% damage is lethal at lower levels regardless of her attack stat.

Base Ginger is a benchwarmer if you have the Costume. If you don't have the costume, she is a niche pick for Titans or fun raid teams. Do not Limit Break (LB2) the base version unless you have the costume to support the stats. There are better modern Nature snipers and supports.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Ginger.
"Pretty much like the Hippo, just with stronger hits but only to a random enemy." — This was the consensus on launch.
"She hits like a truck albeit randomly... most the time she couldn’t pick the correct heroes to kill off."
. "It sounds a massacre," one player noted, confirming that the buffs coexist and create a chaotic screen of flying numbers.

Ginger is the embodiment of "Spray and Pray." When she works, she feels like the most broken hero in the game, deleting enemies one by one as your team fires off specials. When she fails, she hits a minion or a reviving fighter and then dies. Without her costume, she is showing her age, but the core mechanic remains undeniably fun. She puts the "wild" in "wild card."
If you have the costume, she's a star. If you only have the base version, keep her for Titans and fun offense, but keep her away from your defense team.

A comprehensive review of Ginger, the 5-star Nature hero from the Winter Family. We analyze her unique 'Green Hippo' mechanics, random damage output, and viability in the current meta compared to her costume.
Is she still a gift or just a lump of coal?
